data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Harness strictly beats self-consistency when (a) k is odd and candidate base error falls in a band where a meaningful fraction of majority votes are 'fragile' — i.e., the correct answer holds at k but would flip at k-2 — AND (b) critic recall on fragile-correct items exceeds precision times a threshold so that the net fixed-minus-broke is positive. Specifically: e ∈ [1/k, 3/k) for k≥7, critic recall R ≥ 0.6, and critic precision P satisfying R·(1 - SC_majority_margin) > (1-P)·SC_majority_rate, which for k=7, e≈0.25, and SC accuracy≈0.71 requires P > ~0.43.", "mechanism": "Self-consistency accuracy is a step function of k and base error e: at k=7, SC accuracy jumps from ~0.29 (e>3/7) to ~0.71 (e=3/7) to ~0.92 (e=2/7). At the transition band e∈[1/k,3/k), the SC majority is held by a margin of only 1-2 votes. A critic with recall R on fragile-majority items removes some of those correct items. But it also removes a larger absolute number of incorrect-majority items (which dominate at this error band). The net benefit = R·P_false_majority·N_false - FPR·P_true_majority·N_true. Harness strictly beats SC precisely when this net is positive, which requires the critic to be informative (P meaningfully > base_error_rate) and the error band to straddle the SC decision boundary so that both fragile-correct and fragile-incorrect items exist in meaningful numbers.", "scope": {"covers": ["odd k ≥ 7", "base error e ∈ [1/k, 3/k)", "critic precision > base error rate", "critic recall > 0.5 on fragile-majority items"], "excludes": ["k=5 (insufficient separation between SC error bands)", "base error < 1/k (SC near ceiling, nothing to fix)", "base error > 3/k (SC near floor, harness cannot recover)", "critic precision ≤ base error rate (uninformative critic)"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "At k=7, e∈[1/7,3/7), critic R>0.5 and P>0.43, harness accuracy - SC accuracy ≤ 0 with upper 95% CI < 0.03", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "The observed harness improvement at k=7 is fully attributable to changed base error alone (SC accuracy at the same e without filtering matches harness accuracy ±2pp)", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "At k=5, e∈[0.15,0.35], critic R>0.5 and P>0.3, harness strictly beats SC by >3pp", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "The prior refutation (3f11485bf64e) failed because at k=5 the SC error bands are too coarse (e<0.2 → SC≈0.97; e∈[0.2,0.4) → SC≈0.66) and the studied base errors (0.01-0.033) sat deep in the ceiling regime where SC is already ~1.0. The key insight is that harness value emerges only at the SC decision boundary, which for k=5 is too narrow. Increasing k to 7 or 9 widens the transition band and creates more fragile majorities for the critic to act on. The experiment must target this band directly rather than sampling uniformly across difficulty."}
